reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (headless Chrome, real download+run, light+dark, mobile+desktop) — revision 1

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — REAL WASM run: fresh profile → Download (~490 MB) → auto-init → ready, then Xenova/LaBSE embedded the sentences in a Web Worker; 0 console errors, 0px overflow. Cross-lingual cosine matrix rendered real values: 'The meeting is scheduled for Monday morning.' vs its FR/JA/DE translations scored 0.89–0.96 while unrelated distractor lines ('Please remember to water the plants.' / 'El mercado de valores se desplomó esta semana.') scored 0.59–0.77 — meaning, not language. Standalone probe confirmed pipeline('feature-extraction','Xenova/LaBSE') returns dims [2,768]. screenshots: scratchpad-runnability/shots (desktop light+dark, mobile).
rubric.notes: Agent-verified real 768-d dual-encoder embeddings off the main thread.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — output is a fixed-length 768-d numeric vector per sentence (dims [2,768] in probe). Semantic sanity on the bitext-mining panel (practical page): 'Where is the nearest train station?' matched '最寄りの駅はどこですか？' (cosine 0.928, margin 0.343) with a ✓; 'I would like to book a table for two.' matched the German candidate with a ✓ — correct translation retrieval across languages, an unrelated distractor left with a small margin. Not fake: distinct real cosines per pair.
rubric.notes: Agent-verified: 768-d vectors + correct bitext matches with margins.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact source id Xenova/LaBSE (linked to card + sentence-transformers/LaBSE + Google original), task feature-extraction, backend WebAssembly, quantisation q8, ~490 MB. Inference runs in a Web Worker (new Worker('worker.js')). Auto-generated immutable conformance suite (23 assertions) covers declares-model-id / declares-task / declares-backend / declares-quantisation / inference-off-main-thread.

Agent verdict — fresh profile shows 'Download model (~490 MB)'; after load the shared createModelLoader auto-inits to 'Model ready' with a 'Clear cached model' control + storage detail. Model ONNX fetched THROUGH Cache Storage so a returning visit is scannable → auto-init, no re-download. The cached LaBSE profile was reused across page verifications with no re-download.
rubric.notes: Agent-verified auto-init path via shared loader.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — download progress streamed on the loader; a Retry button surfaces on failure; embedding/mining failures route to .status.err text the visitor can read (role=status aria-live). Runs on the WASM path (no GPU) and produced real output, so it just works without WebGPU. Multi-model page loads a second heavier model (NLLB-200, ~900 MB) on demand with its own loader.

Agent verdict — the sentences textarea, Embed & compare, Reset, the bitext source/candidate textareas + Mine translations, the inspect-vector select, and (ladder pages) the k slider, preset chips and pair sets are all real, labelled, keyboard-operable. Clicking Mine embedded both sides and rendered the alignment table. No dead controls.

Agent verdict — the cosine matrix, 2D projection, bitext matches and margins are all derived from the on-device worker's real embeddings (distinct cosines per pair; correct cross-language matches). No canned result anywhere.

conformance images-have-alt + buttons-have-names derived and pass. Status/output are role=status aria-live; the PCA SVG carries role=img + aria-label; matrix/bitext tables use th scope=row/col; every button/label has an accessible name. Wide matrices/tables scroll inside .matrix-scroll (overflow-x:auto) so the body never scrolls horizontally.
rubric.notes: Labelled SVG + scoped table headers; live status.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — desktop 1280×900 (light+dark) reviewed: no horizontal overflow (measured documentElement.scrollWidth−innerWidth = 0px), legible text, at-a-glance table wraps, grid-2 collapses. Mobile 360×740 DPR3 automated-clean (responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean) + agent mobile screenshot reviewed. Wide cosine matrix + bitext table scroll within .matrix-scroll rather than pushing the page.
rubric.notes: Agent-verified: 0px desktop overflow, light+dark clean, mobile matrix scrolls.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — all embedding runs in a Web Worker; the main thread only does small maths (cosine/PCA/k-means/bitext argmax) over a handful of already-computed 768-d vectors. Main thread stayed responsive during the ~490 MB load + inference; 0 console errors.
rubric.notes: Agent-verified off-thread inference; cheap main-thread maths.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy work off the main thread / break up long tasks so inference doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all LaBSE embedding runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; the page only does light cosine/PCA/k-means over a few 768-d vectors.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep the interaction path light so critical work runs first.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — clicking Embed/Mine posts to the worker; the argmax bitext match and matrix render are O(n²) over small n, off the interaction critical path.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: headless: main-thread responsive during 490 MB load + inference; 0 console errors

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid layout; avoid fixed widths; wrap wide content so the body never scrolls horizontally.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — grid-2 uses auto-fit minmax and collapses on mobile; the cosine matrix + bitext table are wrapped in .matrix-scroll (overflow-x:auto); at-a-glance table wraps.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: desktop 1280 measured 0px horizontal overflow; responsive-check mobile clean

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label every control; announce state in a live region; give visualisations accessible names.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live loader + status; PCA SVG has role=img+aria-label; matrix/bitext tables use th scope; labelled buttons/selects/textareas.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (invariant 6); crumbs link back to the catalogue.

openQuestions: LaBSE is ~490 MB — is a WebGPU fp16 fast path worth adding as an additive enhancement for capable devices (honest WASM default retained)?

The dependency-free language guesser only drives the flag badges; on ambiguous short Latin strings it can mislabel a flag while the embedding (the real signal) is still correct — worth noting more prominently?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a fourth wild demo: cross-lingual near-duplicate detection over a larger pasted corpus (collapse same-meaning lines across languages), reusing the LaBSE worker — additive.
Add a WebGPU execution-provider fast path for LaBSE (honest WASM fallback retained) as an additive enhancement for high-memory devices.

summary: Revision-1 agent build+matrix pass for labse-embeddings: real ~490 MB download + on-device 768-d dual-encoder embedding verified in headless Chrome (real cross-lingual cosine matrix — translations 0.89–0.96 vs distractors 0.59–0.77 — and correct bitext-mining matches with margins, e.g. EN→JA train-station 0.928/0.343), light+dark desktop + mobile screenshots reviewed (0px overflow, wide tables scroll in .matrix-scroll), console clean. Distinct from the paraphrase-objective multilingual-MiniLM: LaBSE is the translation-retrieval / bitext-mining embedder. Auto-derived immutable conformance suite (23 assertions); manual-evidenced dimensions carry the agent verdict above.
